hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) electrostatic interactions without compromising SSTR2 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong binding but fail to improve selectivity. The focus must shift to disrupting conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 through targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the pharmacophore (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
These mutations are predicted to break conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the native pharmacophore and cysteine positions.
